stateOverview.title: California — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In California, developmental disability services are delivered through a network of Regional Centers , operated under the Department of Developmental Services (DDS) . Your local Regional Center is your single entry point for all DD services. California's approach is unique — most services flow through the Regional Center system rather than traditional waivers: Regional Center Services — funded by the state; includes early intervention, behavioral support, respite, day programs, employment support, and more. Available regardless of Medicaid status for eligible individuals. HCBS Waiver (Medi-Cal) — for individuals who also have Medi-Cal; covers additional services not funded by Regional Center. IHSS (In-Home Supportive Services) — not a waiver, but provides in-home care hours for eligible Medi-Cal recipients. Often available sooner than waiver services. California Regional Center services are available to individuals diagnosed with autism, intellectual disability, or related conditions before age 18. Medi-Cal (California's Medicaid) is separate from Regional Center enrollment. Apply at BenefitsCal.com . Find your Regional Center → dds.ca.gov

sections.heading: Your Regional Center
sections.content: Los Angeles County has multiple Regional Centers based on your ZIP code: Regional Center of the East Bay (RCEB): 510-383-1200 — East LA areas Frank D. Lanterman Regional Center: 818-244-2452 — San Fernando Valley, Glendale, Pasadena areas Harbor Regional Center: 310-540-1711 — South Bay, Long Beach areas North Los Angeles County Regional Center: 818-778-1900 — North Valley areas South Central Los Angeles Regional Center: 323-543-4900 — South Central LA Westside Regional Center: 310-258-4000 — West LA, Santa Monica areas 🌐 Find your specific RC by ZIP → dds.ca.gov

sections.heading: Regional Center Enrollment
sections.content: Contact your Regional Center to begin the intake and eligibility process. Services can begin even before a formal autism diagnosis — contact them as soon as you have concerns.

sections.heading: Medi-Cal (California Medicaid)
sections.content: Apply at BenefitsCal.com or call 1-800-541-5555. Medi-Cal is separate from Regional Center but needed for HCBS waiver services.

stateOverview.title: Delaware — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Delaware, developmental disability services are administered by the Division of Developmental Disabilities Services (DDDS) , part of the Department of Health and Social Services (DHSS). Delaware has two primary DD waivers: Community Support Waiver — for adults 18 and older with developmental disabilities living in the community. Family Supported Services Waiver — for children and young adults with DD living with family. More accessible with shorter waitlists. Delaware is a small state — DDDS operates centrally with regional offices rather than county-level agencies. ⚠️ Community Support Waiver waitlist: 1–3 years. Family Supported Services: 3–6 months. Delaware Medicaid is called Delaware Medical Assistance Program (DMAP) . Apply through ASSIST . DDDS Delaware → ddds.delaware.gov

kent.address: Dover, Smyrna, Milford (Kent portion), Camden, Harrington
kent.applyInstructions: Contact DDDS Kent County to apply for the Family Supported Services Waiver for children with DD.

waivers.name: Family Supported Services Waiver (Children)
waivers.description: Contact DDDS Kent County to apply for the Family Supported Services Waiver for children with DD.

waivers.name: Community Support Waiver (Adults)
waivers.description: Apply for the Community Support Waiver through DDDS Kent County for adults 18 and older.

sections.heading: Your DDDS Office
sections.content: DDDS Kent County Regional Office serves Kent County. 📞 Phone: 302-744-9600 🌐 Website: ddds.delaware.gov 📍 Service area: Dover, Smyrna, Milford (Kent portion), Camden, Harrington

sections.heading: Family Supported Services Waiver (Children)

stateOverview.title: Kansas — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Kansas, DD services are administered by KDADS through local Community Developmental Disability Organizations (CDDOs). IDD Waiver — covers residential support, day services, supported employment, respite, behavioral support, and transportation. ⚠️ IDD Waiver waitlists: 3–6 years . Kansas Medicaid (KanCare): kancare.ks.gov or 1-800-792-4884. Kansas KDADS → kdads.ks.gov

stateOverview.title: Minnesota — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Minnesota, developmental disability services are administered by the Department of Human Services (DHS) through county human services agencies. Minnesota primary waivers: DD Waiver — for individuals with intellectual disability or related conditions; covers residential support, day services, supported employment, respite, and behavioral support. Brain Injury (BI) Waiver — for individuals with acquired brain injuries. Community Access for Disability Inclusion (CADI) Waiver — for individuals with physical disabilities or those at risk of nursing home placement. Apply through your county human services agency . They conduct needs assessments and manage waiver access. ⚠️ DD Waiver waitlists: 3–7 years . Apply as early as possible. Minnesota Medicaid is called Medical Assistance (MA) . Apply through your county or at MNbenefits.mn.gov . Minnesota DHS DD → mn.gov/dhs

stateOverview.title: Michigan — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Michigan, developmental disability services are delivered through Community Mental Health Service Programs (CMHSPs) and their regional Prepaid Inpatient Health Plans (PIHPs) , under the Michigan Department of Health and Human Services (MDHHS). Michigan primary waiver: Habilitation Supports Waiver (HSW) — covers personal care, respite, day habilitation, supported employment, behavioral support, and transportation. Apply through your local CMHSP. MI Choice Waiver — for adults who would otherwise need nursing facility care. Your local CMHSP is your entry point. They conduct eligibility screening, manage waitlists, and provide service coordination. ⚠️ HSW waitlists: 3–6 years . Apply as early as possible. Michigan Medicaid is called Medicaid . Apply through MI Bridges or call 1-888-642-7434. Find your CMHSP → michigan.gov

stateOverview.title: Montana — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Montana, DD services are administered by the Developmental Disabilities Program (DDP) , part of DPHHS. HCBS DD Waiver — covers personal care, respite, day habilitation, supported employment, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Waitlists: 2–4 years . Montana Medicaid: dphhs.mt.gov or 406-444-4540.

stateOverview.title: Pennsylvania — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Pennsylvania, developmental disability services are administered by the Office of Developmental Programs (ODP) , part of the Department of Human Services. Pennsylvania primary waivers: Consolidated Waiver — the most comprehensive; for adults with intellectual disability or autism needing residential or significant community supports. Person/Family Directed Support (P/FDS) Waiver — for individuals living at home with family; covers day supports, respite, behavioral support, and employment. Community Living Waiver — for adults transitioning to community living. Access begins at your local Administrative Entity (AE) — typically your county's intellectual disability office. The AE manages eligibility and waitlists. ⚠️ Consolidated Waiver waitlists: 3–8 years . P/FDS Waiver: 1–3 years . Apply as early as possible. Pennsylvania Medicaid is called Medical Assistance (MA) . Apply through COMPASS or call 1-866-550-4355. Pennsylvania ODP → dhs.pa.gov/odp

stateOverview.title: Oregon — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Oregon, DD services are administered by the Office of Developmental Disability Services (ODDS) . Oregon uses a brokerage system — once enrolled, a personal agent helps plan services. HCBS DD Waiver — covers residential support, day services, supported employment, respite, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Waitlists: 1–3 years — shorter than most states. Oregon Health Plan (OHP): Apply through ONE Oregon or call 1-800-699-9075. Oregon ODDS → oregon.gov/dhs/DD

stateOverview.title: West Virginia — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In West Virginia, DD services are administered by the Bureau for Behavioral Health (BBH) , part of DHHR. IDD Waiver — covers residential support, day habilitation, supported employment, respite, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Waitlists: 3–6 years . Apply through your local DHHR county office. West Virginia Medicaid: dhhr.wv.gov or 304-558-1700. West Virginia BBH/DD → dhhr.wv.gov

stateOverview.title: New York — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In New York, developmental disability services are administered by the Office for People With Developmental Disabilities (OPWDD) . OPWDD is your single entry point for all DD services and waiver enrollment. New York's primary waiver is the HCBS Waiver for People with Developmental Disabilities : Covers day habilitation, residential support, behavioral support, employment support, respite, community habilitation, and transportation Requires active NY Medicaid and OPWDD eligibility determination Developmental disability must be diagnosed before age 22 New York also has Care Coordination Organizations (CCOs) that help coordinate services once you are enrolled with OPWDD. ⚠️ Waitlists typically 1–3 years . New York is one of the more accessible states for DD services — but apply early. NY Medicaid is called New York Medicaid . Apply through NY State of Health or your local Department of Social Services. OPWDD → opwdd.ny.gov

stateOverview.title: Connecticut — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Connecticut, DD services are administered by the Department of Developmental Services (DDS) with 4 regional offices. Comprehensive Supports Waiver — for individuals needing residential support. Individual Supports Waiver — for individuals living at home. ⚠️ Waitlists: 3–6 years . Connecticut Medicaid (HUSKY Health): ct.gov/dss or 1-855-626-6632. Connecticut DDS → portal.ct.gov/dds

stateOverview.title: Kentucky — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Kentucky, developmental disability services are administered by the Department for Behavioral Health, Developmental and Intellectual Disabilities (DBHDID) , part of the Cabinet for Health and Family Services. Kentucky primary waivers: Michelle P. Waiver (MPW) — Kentucky's primary DD waiver; covers personal care, respite, day training, supported employment, behavioral support, and transportation. Supports for Community Living (SCL) Waiver — for adults with more intensive support needs including residential. Home and Community Based (HCB) Waiver — for individuals transitioning from institutional settings. Apply through your local DBHDID regional office or community mental health center. ⚠️ MPW waitlists: 3–7 years . SCL: 5–10 years . Apply as early as possible. Kentucky Medicaid is called Medicaid / kynect . Apply at kynect.ky.gov or call 1-855-459-6328. Kentucky DBHDID → chfs.ky.gov

stateOverview.title: Oklahoma — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Oklahoma, developmental disability services and the DD Waiver are administered by the Oklahoma Department of Human Services (DHS) through the Developmental Disabilities Services Division (DDSD) . Oklahoma's primary waiver is the Developmental Disabilities Services (DDS) Waiver : Covers residential support, day habilitation, supported employment, respite, behavioral support, and transportation Requires active SoonerCare (Medicaid) and DDSD eligibility determination Apply through your local DHS district office ⚠️ Oklahoma DD waiver waitlists are typically 3–7 years . Apply as early as possible. SoonerCare is Oklahoma's Medicaid program. Apply at okhca.org or call 1-800-987-7767. Oklahoma DDS → okdhs.org

stateOverview.title: Georgia — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Georgia, developmental disability services and the COMP Waiver are administered by the Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Disabilities (DBHDD) . Georgia's primary waiver is the COMP (Community Services) Waiver : Covers residential support, day support, behavioral support, employment support, respite, transportation, and crisis support Requires active Georgia Medicaid and DBHDD eligibility determination Apply through your DBHDD regional field office Georgia also has NOW (New Options Waiver) for individuals with lower support needs. ⚠️ COMP Waiver waitlists: 3–8 years . Apply as early as possible. Your position on the list is based on application date. Georgia Medicaid is called Georgia Medicaid . Apply through Gateway or your local DFCS office. DBHDD → dbhdd.georgia.gov

stateOverview.title: Massachusetts — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Massachusetts, developmental disability services are administered by the Department of Developmental Services (DDS) , part of the Executive Office of Health and Human Services. Massachusetts primary waivers: Residential Supports Waiver — for individuals needing residential support or significant community assistance. Adult Supports Waiver — for adults 18+ living at home; covers day supports, supported employment, respite, and behavioral support. Community Living Waiver — for individuals who want to live independently with supports. Apply through your local DDS area office . Massachusetts has 6 regions with multiple area offices. ⚠️ Residential Supports waitlists: 4–8 years . Adult Supports: 2–4 years . Apply early. Massachusetts Medicaid is called MassHealth . Apply at mass.gov/masshealth or call 1-800-841-2900. Massachusetts DDS → mass.gov/dds

stateOverview.title: North Carolina — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In North Carolina, developmental disability services are administered by the Division of Mental Health, Developmental Disabilities, and Substance Use Services (DMHDDSUS) through local LME-MCOs (Local Management Entities / Managed Care Organizations) . North Carolina primary waiver: Innovations Waiver — NC's primary DD waiver covering residential support, day supports, supported employment, respite, behavioral support, community navigation, and more. Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) Waiver — for individuals with acquired brain injuries. Your local LME-MCO is your entry point. They manage eligibility, waitlists, and service coordination for your county. ⚠️ Innovations Waiver waitlist: 3–8 years . Apply as early as possible. NC has one of the largest DD waitlists in the country. NC Medicaid is called NC Medicaid . Apply through your county DSS office or online at ePASS NC . Find your LME-MCO → ncdhhs.gov

stateOverview.title: North Dakota — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In North Dakota, DD services are administered by the Division of Developmental Disabilities , part of DHS. North Dakota has shorter waitlists than many states. HCBS DD Waiver — covers personal care, respite, day habilitation, supported employment, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Waitlists: 6–18 months — more accessible than most states. North Dakota Medicaid: hhs.nd.gov or 701-328-2321.

stateOverview.title: New Jersey — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In New Jersey, developmental disability services are administered by the Division of Developmental Disabilities (DDD) , part of the Department of Human Services. New Jersey's primary programs include: Supports Program — for individuals living at home or independently; covers day supports, supported employment, respite, behavioral support. More accessible — shorter waits. Community Care Program — for individuals needing residential support; longer waitlist. Transitional Services Program — for individuals moving from institutional to community settings. ⚠️ Community Care Program waitlists: 2–5 years. Supports Program: 6–18 months. New Jersey Medicaid is called NJ FamilyCare . Apply at njfamilycare.org or call 1-800-701-0710. DDD eligibility requires documentation of intellectual disability, autism, or related condition diagnosed before age 22. NJ DDD → nj.gov/humanservices/ddd

stateOverview.title: New Hampshire — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In New Hampshire, DD services are administered by the Bureau of Developmental Services , part of DHHS. NH uses a regional Area Agency system. stateOverview.title: Nevada — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Nevada, DD services are administered by the Aging and Disability Services Division (ADSD) . Developmental Services HCBS Waiver — covers personal care, respite, day habilitation, supported employment, behavioral support, and transportation. ⚠️ Waitlists: 2–5 years . Nevada Medicaid: Nevada DWSS or 1-800-992-0900. Nevada ADSD → adsd.nv.gov

stateOverview.title: Texas — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Texas, long-term services and supports (LTSS) for individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD) — including autism — are managed by the Health and Human Services Commission (HHSC) and accessed through your local LIDDA (Local Intellectual and Developmental Disability Authority) . Texas has several waiver programs. The most relevant for autism and IDD families are: HCS (Home and Community-based Services) — the most comprehensive waiver; covers residential supports, day habilitation, supported employment, and more. Long waitlist. TxHmL (Texas Home Living) — less intensive than HCS; for individuals living in their own home or with family. Also waitlisted. CLASS (Community Living Assistance and Support Services) — for individuals with related conditions affecting brain function. ⚠️ Waitlists are very long — HCS waitlists can be 10 to 15 or more years in many parts of Texas. Apply as early as possible. Your first step is always contacting your local LIDDA . Find your LIDDA → hhs.texas.gov

stateOverview.title: Alabama — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Alabama, DD services are administered through the Alabama Medicaid Agency and Department of Rehabilitation Services (ADRS) . HCBS DD Waiver — covers personal care, respite, day habilitation, supported employment, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Waitlists: 2–5 years . Alabama Medicaid: medicaid.alabama.gov or 1-800-362-1504. Alabama ADRS → rehab.alabama.gov

stateOverview.title: Virginia — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Virginia, Medicaid waiver services for individuals with developmental disabilities — including autism — are administered by the Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Services (DBHDS) and funded through Virginia Medicaid (managed by DMAS ). Virginia has three primary DD waivers: Building Independence (BI) Waiver — for adults 18+ who are relatively independent. Family and Individual Supports (FIS) Waiver — for children and adults living with family; covers respite, supported employment, crisis services. Community Living (CL) Waiver — for individuals needing more intensive residential support. Access begins at your local Community Services Board (CSB) . The CSB conducts eligibility screening and manages the waiver waiting list. ⚠️ Virginia DD waivers have waiting lists. Apply early — your child does not need to be in crisis to get on the list. Find your CSB → dbhds.virginia.gov

stateOverview.title: Colorado — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Colorado, Medicaid waiver services for individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD) — including autism — are administered by the Colorado Department of Health Care Policy and Financing (HCPF) and the Colorado Department of Human Services (CDHS) . Colorado has two primary DD waivers: Children's Extensive Support (CES) Waiver — for children under 18 with significant developmental disabilities. Covers respite, behavioral support, homemaker/home health aide, specialized equipment, day habilitation, crisis intervention, and transportation. Supported Living Services (SLS) Waiver — for adults 18 and older. Covers personal care, supported living, day habilitation, transportation, behavioral support, respite, and employment support. Access to both waivers begins at your local Community Centered Board (CCB) . The CCB is your single entry point for all developmental disability services in Colorado — they conduct eligibility screening, manage waiver waitlists, and provide service coordination. ⚠️ Waitlists apply — CES waiver typically 2–4 years, SLS waiver 5–10+ years. Apply as early as possible. Your child does not need to be in crisis to get on the list. Colorado Medicaid (Health First Colorado) is a separate application from the waiver and should be applied for first at peak.colorado.gov . Find your CCB → cdhs.colorado.gov

stateOverview.title: Vermont — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Vermont, DD services are administered by the Division of Disability and Aging Services (DAIL) . Vermont uses a Designated Agency system. Developmental Services HCBS Waiver — covers residential support, day services, supported employment, respite, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Waitlists: 1–2 years — one of the most accessible states. Vermont Medicaid (Green Mountain Care): greenmountaincare.vermont.gov or 1-800-250-8427. Vermont DAIL → dail.vermont.gov

stateOverview.title: Utah — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Utah, DD services are administered by the Division of Services for People with Disabilities (DSPD) . Medicaid HCBS DD Waiver — covers residential support, day habilitation, supported employment, respite, behavioral support, and transportation. ⚠️ DSPD waitlists: 3–6 years . Utah Medicaid: medicaid.utah.gov or 801-538-6155. Utah DSPD → dspd.utah.gov

stateOverview.title: Mississippi — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Mississippi, DD services are administered through the Department of Mental Health (DMH) and regional mental health centers. ID/DD Waiver — covers residential support, day services, supported employment, respite, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Mississippi waitlists: 3–8 years . Apply as early as possible. Mississippi Medicaid: medicaid.ms.gov or 601-359-6050.

stateOverview.title: Hawaii — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Hawaii, DD services are administered by the Division on Developmental Disabilities (DDD) , part of the Department of Health. HCBS DD Waiver — covers personal care, respite, day habilitation, supported employment, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Waitlists: 2–5 years . Hawaii Medicaid (Med-QUEST): medquest.hawaii.gov or 808-524-3370. Hawaii DDD → health.hawaii.gov/ddd

stateOverview.title: Wisconsin — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Wisconsin, developmental disability services are administered by the Department of Health Services (DHS) through a managed care system. Wisconsin primary programs: Family Care Waiver — for adults 18+ with DD; delivered through Managed Care Organizations (MCOs). Covers residential support, day services, supported employment, respite, and behavioral support. One of the more accessible systems. Children's Long-Term Support (CLTS) Waiver — for children under 22 with DD; covers respite, day services, behavioral support, and assistive technology. Shorter waitlists. IRIS (Include, Respect, I Self-Direct) — self-directed option for adults who want to manage their own supports. Apply through your local Aging and Disability Resource Center (ADRC) — they screen for eligibility and connect you to the right MCO. ⚠️ Family Care waitlists: 1–3 years . CLTS: 6–18 months . Wisconsin Medicaid is called ForwardHealth . Apply through ACCESS Wisconsin or call 1-800-362-3002. Wisconsin Family Care → dhs.wisconsin.gov

stateOverview.title: Ohio — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Ohio, developmental disability services are administered by the Department of Developmental Disabilities (DODD) through a network of County Boards of Developmental Disabilities . Ohio primary waivers: Level One Waiver — for individuals with DD living at home; covers day supports, respite, behavioral support, employment, and community inclusion. Most accessible. SELF Waiver — self-directed services for individuals who want more control over their supports. Individual Options (IO) Waiver — for individuals with more intensive needs including residential support. Your local County Board of DD is your entry point — they conduct eligibility evaluations, manage waitlists, and coordinate services. ⚠️ Level One Waiver waitlists: 1–3 years . IO Waiver: 3–6 years . Apply early through your county board. Ohio Medicaid is administered through Ohio Benefits . Apply at benefits.ohio.gov or call 1-800-324-8680. Find your County Board → dodd.ohio.gov

stateOverview.title: Maine — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Maine, DD services are administered by the Office of Aging and Disability Services (OADS) . HCBS DD Waiver — covers personal support, respite, day habilitation, supported employment, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Waitlists: 2–4 years . Maine Medicaid (MaineCare): maine.gov/dhhs or 1-800-977-6740. Maine OADS → maine.gov/dhhs/oads

stateOverview.title: South Dakota — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In South Dakota, DD services are administered by the Division of Developmental Disabilities (DDS) , part of DSS. South Dakota has shorter waitlists than many states. HCBS DD Waiver — covers personal care, respite, day habilitation, supported employment, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Waitlists: 6–18 months — more accessible than most states. South Dakota Medicaid: dss.sd.gov/medicaid or 605-773-3495.

sections.heading: How to Apply
sections.content: South Dakota DD services are state administered, so there is no county level agency. The Division of Developmental Disabilities is the single point of entry for all counties. Call Dakota at Home and press option 2 for DDD.

stateOverview.title: Idaho — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Idaho, DD services are administered by the Division of Developmental Disabilities , part of IDHW. DD HCBS Waiver — covers personal care, respite, day habilitation, supported employment, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Waitlists: 2–4 years . Idaho Medicaid: healthandwelfare.idaho.gov or 1-800-926-2588.

stateOverview.title: Arkansas — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Arkansas, DD services are administered by the Division of Developmental Disabilities Services (DDDS) . ARChoices in Homecare Waiver — covers personal care, respite, day habilitation, supported employment, and behavioral support. ⚠️ ARChoices waitlists: 2–4 years . Arkansas Medicaid: humanservices.arkansas.gov or 1-800-482-5431.

stateOverview.title: Louisiana — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Louisiana, DD services are administered by the Office for Citizens with Developmental Disabilities (OCDD) . New Opportunities Waiver (NOW) — Louisiana primary DD waiver; covers residential support, day habilitation, supported employment, respite, and behavioral support. ⚠️ NOW Waiver waitlists: 5–10+ years — among the longest in the US. Apply immediately. Louisiana Medicaid: myplan.healthylouisiana.com or 1-888-342-6207. Louisiana OCDD → ldh.la.gov

stateOverview.title: Washington — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Washington, developmental disability services are administered by the Developmental Disabilities Administration (DDA) , part of DSHS. Washington has one of the better-funded DD systems in the country. Washington primary waivers: Core Waiver — most comprehensive; covers residential support, day habilitation, supported employment, respite, behavioral support, and transportation. Basic Plus Waiver — for individuals with lower support needs living at home. Community Protection Waiver — for individuals with specific behavioral needs. Apply through your local DDA office . Washington's waits are shorter than many states — typically 1–3 years . Washington Medicaid is called Apple Health . Apply at wahealthplanfinder.org or call 1-855-923-4633. Washington DDA → dshs.wa.gov/dda

stateOverview.title: Missouri — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Missouri, developmental disability services are administered by the Division of Developmental Disabilities (DMH/DD) , part of the Department of Mental Health. Missouri primary waivers: Comprehensive Waiver — for individuals with DD needing residential or intensive community supports. Support Waiver — for individuals living at home; covers day services, supported employment, respite, and behavioral support. More accessible than comprehensive. Missouri Children with Developmental Disabilities (MOCDD) Waiver — for children with DD. Apply through your local DMH regional office . Missouri has regional offices covering the state. ⚠️ Comprehensive Waiver waitlists: 3–7 years . Support Waiver: 1–3 years . Missouri Medicaid is called MO HealthNet . Apply at mydss.mo.gov or call 1-855-373-9994. Missouri DMH/DD → dmh.mo.gov

stateOverview.title: Rhode Island — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Rhode Island, DD services are administered by the Division on Developmental Disabilities (BHDDH) . HCBS DD Waiver — covers residential support, day habilitation, supported employment, respite, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Waitlists: 2–5 years . Rhode Island is a small state — apply through the state office directly. Rhode Island Medicaid: healthyrhode.ri.gov or 401-462-5300. Rhode Island BHDDH → bhddh.ri.gov

stateOverview.title: Arizona — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Arizona, developmental disability services are administered by the Division of Developmental Disabilities (DDD) , part of the Department of Economic Security (DES). Arizona uses AHCCCS (Medicaid) to fund most DD services. Arizona primary programs: DDD HCBS Waiver — covers habilitation, respite, supported employment, day programs, residential support, behavioral support, and transportation. Arizona Long Term Care System (ALTCS) — for individuals who need nursing facility level of care; includes DD services through DDD. Registration with DDD is your first step — before you can access any waiver services. Waitlists are typically 6–18 months — shorter than many states. Arizona Medicaid is called AHCCCS . Apply at healthearizonaplus.gov or call 602-417-4000. Arizona DDD → des.az.gov/ddd

apache_county.applyInstructions: The Arizona Division of Developmental Disabilities (DDD) provides services statewide. Regional Program Managers oversee different areas (West, East, Central, North, South), but specific county assignments for these managers are not publicly detailed. DDD supports individuals with Autism, Cerebral palsy, Epilepsy, Cognitive / Intellectual Disability, Down Syndrome, or those under six at risk of a Developmental Disability.

stateOverview.title: Iowa — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Iowa, DD services are administered through Mental Health and Disability Services (MHDS) regions. Intellectual Disability (ID) Waiver — covers residential support, day habilitation, supported employment, respite, and behavioral support. ⚠️ ID Waiver waitlists: 2–5 years . Iowa Medicaid: hhs.iowa.gov or 1-800-338-8366. Iowa MHDS → hhs.iowa.gov

sections.heading: ID Waiver Application

stateOverview.title: Wyoming — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Wyoming, DD services are administered by the Division of Developmental Disabilities (DDD) , part of the Department of Health. Wyoming has shorter waitlists due to the smaller population. HCBS DD Waiver — covers personal care, respite, day habilitation, supported employment, and behavioral support. ⚠️ Waitlists: 6–18 months — more accessible than most states. Wyoming Medicaid: health.wyo.gov or 307-777-7531. Wyoming DDD → health.wyo.gov/ddd

stateOverview.title: Washington D.C. — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Washington D.C., DD services are administered by the Department on Disability Services (DDS) . Medicaid Waiver for Persons with IDD — covers residential support, day habilitation, supported employment, respite, behavioral support, and transportation. ⚠️ Waitlists: 2–5 years . D.C. is a single jurisdiction — apply directly through DDS. D.C. Medicaid: dhcf.dc.gov or 202-442-5988. D.C. DDS → dds.dc.gov

dc.applyInstructions: Contact D.C. DDS to begin eligibility screening and waiver enrollment. D.C. is a single jurisdiction — there is one office for all residents.

stateOverview.title: Florida — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Florida, developmental disability services and the iBudget Waiver are administered by the Agency for Persons with Disabilities (APD) . APD is your primary entry point for all DD services, waiver applications, and service coordination. Florida's primary waiver is the iBudget Waiver : Uses a person-centered budget model — individuals receive an annual budget amount to purchase approved services Covers supported living, day training, behavioral analysis, respite, supported employment, transportation, and therapies Requires enrollment with APD and active Florida Medicaid ⚠️ Waitlists apply — typically 5–10 years. Florida has one of the largest iBudget waitlists in the country. Apply as early as possible. Florida Medicaid is administered through the Agency for Health Care Administration (AHCA) . Apply through ACCESS Florida . Autism diagnosis must be documented by age 18 to qualify for APD eligibility. APD Florida → apd.myflorida.com

stateOverview.title: Tennessee — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Tennessee, developmental disability services are administered by the Department of Intellectual and Developmental Disabilities (DIDD) . Tennessee primary waiver: Supports Waiver — for individuals with IDD living at home; covers day supports, supported employment, respite, behavioral support, and community participation. Employment and Community First (ECF) CHOICES — for individuals with IDD who also need long-term supports; covers residential, day, and employment services. Apply through your DIDD regional office . Tennessee has 4 regions covering the state. ⚠️ Waiver waitlists: 4–8 years . Apply early — Tennessee has significant demand. Tennessee Medicaid is called TennCare . Apply at tn.gov/tenncare or call 1-800-342-3145. Tennessee DIDD → tn.gov/didd

anderson.agencyName: TN Department of Disability and Aging, East Tennessee Regional Office
anderson.applyInstructions: Tennessee waivers (including Katie Beckett and ECF CHOICES) are administered by the state Department of Disability and Aging through three regional offices, not county agencies. Any regional office can redirect you if your county sits near a boundary. Katie Beckett applications go through TennCare Connect.

Any regional office can redirect you if your county sits near a boundary. Katie Beckett applications go through TennCare Connect.

stateOverview.title: New Mexico — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In New Mexico, DD services are administered by the Developmental Disabilities Support Division (DDSD) . DD Waiver — covers residential support, day habilitation, supported employment, respite, behavioral support, and transportation. Mi Via Waiver — self-directed option. ⚠️ DD Waiver waitlists: 3–6 years . New Mexico Medicaid (Centennial Care): hsd.state.nm.us or 1-888-997-2583. New Mexico DDSD → nmhealth.org

bernalillo.applyInstructions: Contact DDSD Metro Region to apply for DD waiver services in Bernalillo County.

stateOverview.title: Illinois — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Illinois, developmental disability services are administered by the Division of Developmental Disabilities (DDD) , part of the Department of Human Services (IDHS). Illinois primary waivers: Home-Based Support Services (HBSS) Waiver — for individuals with DD living at home with family. Covers day training, behavioral support, respite, supported employment, and assistive technology. Community Integrated Living Arrangements (CILA) — residential support for adults with DD. Supported Living Waiver — for adults who want to live independently with supports. ⚠️ Illinois waiver waitlists are typically 3–7 years . Apply as early as possible through your local DDD office. Illinois Medicaid is called Medical Assistance . Apply through ABE Benefits portal or call 1-800-843-6154. Illinois DDD → dhs.state.il.us

stateOverview.title: South Carolina — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In South Carolina, developmental disability services are administered by the Department of Disabilities and Special Needs (DDSN) . South Carolina primary waivers: Intellectual Disability / Related Disabilities (ID/RD) Waiver — for individuals with intellectual disability or autism; covers residential support, day habilitation, supported employment, respite, and behavioral support. Community Supports Waiver — for individuals with lower support needs living at home. Apply through your local DDSN district office . South Carolina has 4 districts. ⚠️ ID/RD Waiver waitlists: 3–7 years . Apply as early as possible. South Carolina Medicaid is called South Carolina Medicaid (SCDHHS) . Apply at scdhhs.gov or call 1-888-549-0820. South Carolina DDSN → ddsn.sc.gov

stateOverview.title: Maryland — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Maryland, developmental disability services are administered by the Developmental Disabilities Administration (DDA) , part of the Maryland Department of Health. Maryland primary waivers: Community Pathways Waiver — most comprehensive; for individuals needing residential or significant community supports. Community Supports Waiver — for individuals living at home; covers day supports, respite, supported employment, and behavioral support. Family Supports Waiver — for children and young adults living with family. Apply through your local DDA regional office . Maryland has 5 regions. ⚠️ Community Pathways waitlists: 3–6 years . Family Supports: 1–2 years . Maryland Medicaid is called HealthChoice . Apply at mmcp.health.maryland.gov or call 1-800-492-5231. Maryland DDA → dda.health.maryland.gov

stateOverview.title: Indiana — Medicaid & Waiver Overview
stateOverview.body: In Indiana, developmental disability services are administered by the Bureau of Developmental Disability Services (BDDS) , part of the Family and Social Services Administration (FSSA). Indiana primary waivers: Community Integration and Habilitation (CIH) Waiver — most comprehensive; for individuals needing residential support or intensive community services. Family Supports Waiver (FSW) — for individuals with DD living at home with family; covers respite, behavioral support, day services, and supported employment. More accessible. Support Services Waiver — for individuals with lower support needs. Apply through your local BDDS district office . Indiana has 5 districts. ⚠️ CIH Waiver waitlists: 3–7 years . FSW: 1–3 years . Indiana Medicaid is called Medicaid . Apply at in.gov/fssa or call 1-800-403-0864. Indiana BDDS → in.gov/fssa/ddrs
